title: Greetings in Context — First Listen
description: Listen to a native Gujarati greeting exchange and practice comprehension exercises.
audioTranscript: Rahul: નમસ્તે! Sneha: નમસ્તે! કેમ છો? Rahul: હું સારો છું, આભાર. Sneha: ખૂબ સારું!

blocks.title: Listening Notes — Before You Listen
blocks.instruction: Listen to the Gujarati greeting dialogue and take notes as you go:

sections.label: How many speakers did you hear?
Which greeting words did you catch?

items.statement: Gujarati speakers say 'Namaste' to greet someone.
items.explanation: 'Namaste' (નમસ્તે) is the standard Gujarati greeting — used for hello AND goodbye.

items.statement: 'Kem chho?' is used to say goodbye.
items.explanation: 'Kem chho?' (કેમ છો?) means 'How are you?' It's a question, not a farewell!

items.statement: 'Saaro chhun, aabhaar' is a typical response to 'Kem chho?'
items.explanation: 'સારો/સારી છું, આભાર' = 'I am fine, thank you' — the standard polite response.

blocks.title: The Sound of Gujarati
blocks.content: Gujarati is known for its melodic, rhythmic quality. The language has a musical cadence that many learners find beautiful. It's spoken by over 60 million people across Gujarat, Mumbai, UK, USA, and East Africa. Famous speakers include Mahatma Gandhi — Gujarati was his mother tongue!
